kjv.NUM_28_15: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering unto the Lord shall be offered, beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_28_16: And in the fourteenth day of the first month is the passover of the Lord.
kjv.NUM_28_17: And in the fifteenth day of this month is the feast: seven days shall unleavened bread be eaten.
kjv.NUM_28_18: In the first day shall be an holy convocation; ye shall do no manner of servile work therein:
kjv.NUM_28_19: But ye shall offer a sacrifice made by fire for a burnt offering unto the Lord; two young bullocks, and one ram, and seven lambs of the first year: they shall be unto you without blemish:
kjv.NUM_28_20: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil: three tenth deals shall ye offer for a bullock, and two tenth deals for a ram;
kjv.NUM_28_21: A several tenth deal shalt thou offer for every lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
kjv.NUM_28_22: And one goat for a sin offering, to make an atonement for you.
kjv.NUM_28_23: Ye shall offer these beside the burnt offering in the morning, which is for a continual burnt offering.
kjv.NUM_28_24: After this manner ye shall offer daily, throughout the seven days, the meat of the sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord: it shall be offered beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_28_25: And on the seventh day ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work.
kjv.NUM_28_26: Also in the day of the firstfruits, when ye bring a new meat offering unto the Lord, after your weeks be out, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work:
kjv.NUM_28_27: But ye shall offer the burnt offering for a sweet savour unto the Lord; two young bullocks, one ram, seven lambs of the first year;
kjv.NUM_28_28: And their meat offering of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals unto one bullock, two tenth deals unto one ram,
kjv.NUM_28_29: A several tenth deal unto one lamb, throughout the seven lambs;
kjv.NUM_28_30: And one kid of the goats, to make an atonement for you.
kjv.NUM_28_31: Ye shall offer them beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, (they shall be unto you without blemish) and their drink offerings.
kjv.NUM_29_1: And in the seventh month, on the first day of the month, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work: it is a day of blowing the trumpets unto you.
kjv.NUM_29_2: And ye shall offer a burnt offering for a sweet savour unto the Lord; one young bullock, one ram, and seven lambs of the first year without blemish:
kjv.NUM_29_3: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals for a bullock, and two tenth deals for a ram,
kjv.NUM_29_4: And one tenth deal for one lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
kjv.NUM_29_5: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering, to make an atonement for you:
kjv.NUM_29_6: Beside the burnt offering of the month, and his meat offering, and the daily burnt offering, and his meat offering, and their drink offerings, according unto their manner, for a sweet savour, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord.
kjv.NUM_29_7: And ye shall have on the tenth day of this seventh month an holy convocation; and ye shall afflict your souls: ye shall not do any work therein:
kjv.NUM_29_8: But ye shall offer a burnt offering unto the Lord for a sweet savour; one young bullock, one ram, and seven lambs of the first year; they shall be unto you without blemish:
kjv.NUM_29_9: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals to a bullock, and two tenth deals to one ram,
kjv.NUM_29_10: A several tenth deal for one lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
kjv.NUM_29_11: One kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the sin offering of atonement, and the continual burnt offering, and the meat offering of it, and their drink offerings.
kjv.NUM_29_12: And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work, and ye shall keep a feast unto the Lord seven days:
kjv.NUM_29_13: And ye shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord; thirteen young bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year; they shall be without blemish:
kjv.NUM_29_14: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals unto every bullock of the thirteen bullocks, two tenth deals to each ram of the two rams,
kjv.NUM_29_15: And a several tenth deal to each lamb of the fourteen lambs:
kjv.NUM_29_16: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_29_17: And on the second day ye shall offer twelve young bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year without spot:
kjv.NUM_29_18: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
kjv.NUM_29_19: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and the meat offering thereof, and their drink offerings.
kjv.NUM_29_20: And on the third day eleven bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish;
kjv.NUM_29_21: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
kjv.NUM_29_22: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_29_23: And on the fourth day ten bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
kjv.NUM_29_24: Their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
kjv.NUM_29_25: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_29_26: And on the fifth day nine bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without spot:
kjv.NUM_29_27: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
kjv.NUM_29_28: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_29_29: And on the sixth day eight bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
kjv.NUM_29_30: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
kjv.NUM_29_31: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_29_32: And on the seventh day seven bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
kjv.NUM_29_33: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
kjv.NUM_29_34: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_29_35: On the eighth day ye shall have a solemn assembly: ye shall do no servile work therein:
kjv.NUM_29_36: But ye shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord: one bullock, one ram, seven lambs of the first year without blemish:
kjv.NUM_29_37: Their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullock, for the ram, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
kjv.NUM_29_38: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
kjv.NUM_29_39: These things ye shall do unto the Lord in your set feasts, beside your vows, and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your meat offerings, and for your drink offerings, and for your peace offerings.
kjv.NUM_29_40: And Moses told the children of Israel according to all that the Lord commanded Moses.
kjv.NUM_30_1: And Moses spake unto the heads of the tribes concerning the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the Lord hath commanded.
kjv.NUM_30_2: If a man vow a vow unto the Lord, or swear an oath to bind his soul with a bond; he shall not break his word, he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth.
kjv.NUM_30_3: If a woman also vow a vow unto the Lord, and bind herself by a bond, being in her father's house in her youth;
kjv.NUM_30_4: And her father hear her vow, and her bond wherewith she hath bound her soul, and her father shall hold his peace at her; then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.
kjv.NUM_30_5: But if her father disallow her in the day that he heareth; not any of her vows, or of her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul, shall stand: and the Lord shall forgive her, because her father disallowed her.
kjv.NUM_30_6: And if she had at all an husband, when she vowed, or uttered ought out of her lips, wherewith she bound her soul;
kjv.NUM_30_7: And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her in the day that he heard it: then her vows shall stand, and her bonds wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
kjv.NUM_30_8: But if her husband disallowed her on the day that he heard it; then he shall make her vow which she vowed, and that which she uttered with her lips, wherewith she bound her soul, of none effect: and the Lord shall forgive her.
kjv.NUM_30_9: But every vow of a widow, and of her that is divorced, wherewith they have bound their souls, shall stand against her.
kjv.NUM_30_10: And if she vowed in her husband's house, or bound her soul by a bond with an oath;
kjv.NUM_30_11: And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her, and disallowed her not: then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
kjv.NUM_30_12: But if her husband hath utterly made them void on the day he heard them; then whatsoever proceeded out of her lips concerning her vows, or concerning the bond of her soul, shall not stand: her husband hath made them void; and the Lord shall forgive her.
kjv.NUM_30_13: Every vow, and every binding oath to afflict the soul, her husband may establish it, or her husband may make it void.
kjv.NUM_30_14: But if her husband altogether hold his peace at her from day to day; then he establisheth all her vows, or all her bonds, which are upon her: he confirmeth them, because he held his peace at her in the day that he heard them.
kjv.NUM_30_15: But if he shall any ways make them void after that he hath heard them; then he shall bear her iniquity.
kjv.NUM_30_16: These are the statutes, which the Lord commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between the father and his daughter, being yet in her youth in her father's house.
kjv.NUM_31_1: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
kjv.NUM_31_2: Avenge the children of Israel of the Midianites: afterward shalt thou be gathered unto thy people.
kjv.NUM_31_3: And Moses spake unto the people, saying, Arm some of yourselves unto the war, and let them go against the Midianites, and avenge the Lord of Midian.
kjv.NUM_31_4: Of every tribe a thousand, throughout all the tribes of Israel, shall ye send to the war.
kjv.NUM_31_5: So there were delivered out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand of every tribe, twelve thousand armed for war.
kjv.NUM_31_6: And Moses sent them to the war, a thousand of every tribe, them and Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the holy instruments, and the trumpets to blow in his hand.
kjv.NUM_31_7: And they warred against the Midianites, as the Lord commanded Moses; and they slew all the males.
kjv.NUM_31_8: And they slew the kings of Midian, beside the rest of them that were slain; namely, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, five kings of Midian: Balaam also the son of Beor they slew with the sword.
kjv.NUM_31_9: And the children of Israel took all the women of Midian captives, and their little ones, and took the spoil of all their cattle, and all their flocks, and all their goods.
kjv.NUM_31_10: And they burnt all their cities wherein they dwelt, and all their goodly castles, with fire.
kjv.NUM_31_11: And they took all the spoil, and all the prey, both of men and of beasts.
kjv.NUM_31_12: And they brought the captives, and the prey, and the spoil, unto Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and unto the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the camp at the plains of Moab, which are by Jordan near Jericho.
kjv.NUM_31_13: And Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and all the princes of the congregation, went forth to meet them without the camp.
kjv.NUM_31_14: And Moses was wroth with the officers of the host, with the captains over thousands, and captains over hundreds, which came from the battle.
kjv.NUM_31_15: And Moses said unto them, Have ye saved all the women alive?
kjv.NUM_31_16: Behold, these caused the children of Israel, through the counsel of Balaam, to commit trespass against the Lord in the matter of Peor, and there was a plague among the congregation of the Lord.
kjv.NUM_31_17: Now therefore kill every male among the little ones, and kill every woman that hath known man by lying with him.
kjv.NUM_31_18: But all the women children, that have not known a man by lying with him, keep alive for yourselves.
kjv.NUM_31_19: And do ye abide without the camp seven days: whosoever hath killed any person, and whosoever hath touched any slain, purify both yourselves and your captives on the third day, and on the seventh day.
kjv.NUM_31_20: And purify all your raiment, and all that is made of skins, and all work of goats' hair, and all things made of wood.
kjv.NUM_31_21: And Eleazar the priest said unto the men of war which went to the battle, This is the ordinance of the law which the Lord commanded Moses;
kjv.NUM_31_22: Only the gold, and the silver, the brass, the iron, the tin, and the lead,
kjv.NUM_31_23: Every thing that may abide the fire, ye shall make it go through the fire, and it shall be clean: nevertheless it shall be purified with the water of separation: and all that abideth not the fire ye shall make go through the water.
kjv.NUM_31_24: And ye shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and ye shall be clean, and afterward ye shall come into the camp.
kjv.NUM_31_25: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
kjv.NUM_31_26: Take the sum of the prey that was taken, both of man and of beast, thou, and Eleazar the priest, and the chief fathers of the congregation:
kjv.NUM_31_27: And divide the prey into two parts; between them that took the war upon them, who went out to battle, and between all the congregation:
kjv.NUM_31_28: And levy a tribute unto the Lord of the men of war which went out to battle: one soul of five hundred, both of the persons, and of the beeves, and of the asses, and of the sheep:
kjv.NUM_31_29: Take it of their half, and give it unto Eleazar the priest, for an heave offering of the Lord.
kjv.NUM_31_30: And of the children of Israel's half, thou shalt take one portion of fifty, of the persons, of the beeves, of the asses, and of the flocks, of all manner of beasts, and give them unto the Levites, which keep the charge of the tabernacle of the Lord.
kjv.NUM_31_31: And Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord commanded Moses.
kjv.NUM_31_32: And the booty, being the rest of the prey which the men of war had caught, was six hundred thousand and seventy thousand and five thousand sheep,
kjv.NUM_31_33: And threescore and twelve thousand beeves,
kjv.NUM_31_34: And threescore and one thousand asses,
kjv.NUM_31_35: And thirty and two thousand persons in all, of women that had not known man by lying with him.
kjv.NUM_31_36: And the half, which was the portion of them that went out to war, was in number three hundred thousand and seven and thirty thousand and five hundred sheep:
kjv.NUM_31_37: And the Lord'S tribute of the sheep was six hundred and threescore and fifteen.
kjv.NUM_31_38: And the beeves were thirty and six thousand; of which the Lord'S tribute was threescore and twelve.
kjv.NUM_31_39: And the asses were thirty thousand and five hundred; of which the Lord'S tribute was threescore and one.
kjv.NUM_31_40: And the persons were sixteen thousand; of which the Lord'S tribute was thirty and two persons.
kjv.NUM_31_41: And Moses gave the tribute, which was the Lord'S heave offering, unto Eleazar the priest, as the Lord commanded Moses.
kjv.NUM_31_42: And of the children of Israel's half, which Moses divided from the men that warred,
kjv.NUM_31_43: (Now the half that pertained unto the congregation was three hundred thousand and thirty thousand and seven thousand and five hundred sheep,
kjv.NUM_31_44: And thirty and six thousand beeves,
kjv.NUM_31_45: And thirty thousand asses and five hundred,
kjv.NUM_31_47: Even of the children of Israel's half, Moses took one portion of fifty, both of man and of beast, and gave them unto the Levites, which kept the charge of the tabernacle of the Lord; as the Lord commanded Moses.
kjv.NUM_31_48: And the officers which were over thousands of the host, the captains of thousands, and captains of hundreds, came near unto Moses:
kjv.NUM_31_49: And they said unto Moses, Thy servants have taken the sum of the men of war which are under our charge, and there lacketh not one man of us.
kjv.NUM_31_50: We have therefore brought an oblation for the Lord, what every man hath gotten, of jewels of gold, chains, and bracelets, rings, earrings, and tablets, to make an atonement for our souls before the Lord.
kjv.NUM_31_51: And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of them, even all wrought jewels.
kjv.NUM_31_52: And all the gold of the offering that they offered up to the Lord, of the captains of thousands, and of the captains of hundreds, was sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels.
kjv.NUM_31_53: (For the men of war had taken spoil, every man for himself.)
kjv.NUM_31_54: And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of the captains of thousands and of hundreds, and brought it into the tabernacle of the congregation, for a memorial for the children of Israel before the Lord.
kjv.NUM_32_1: Now the children of Reuben and the children of Gad had a very great multitude of cattle: and when they saw the land of Jazer, and the land of Gilead, that, behold, the place was a place for cattle;
kjv.NUM_32_2: The children of Gad and the children of Reuben came and spake unto Moses, and to Eleazar the priest, and unto the princes of the congregation, saying,
kjv.NUM_32_3: Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Shebam, and Nebo, and Beon,
kjv.NUM_32_4: Even the country which the Lord smote before the congregation of Israel, is a land for cattle, and thy servants have cattle:
kjv.NUM_32_5: Wherefore, said they, if we have found grace in thy sight, let this land be given unto thy servants for a possession, and bring us not over Jordan.
kjv.NUM_32_6: And Moses said unto the children of Gad and to the children of Reuben, Shall your brethren go to war, and shall ye sit here?
kjv.NUM_32_7: And wherefore discourage ye the heart of the children of Israel from going over into the land which the Lord hath given them?
kjv.NUM_32_8: Thus did your fathers, when I sent them from Kadeshbarnea to see the land.
kjv.NUM_32_9: For when they went up unto the valley of Eshcol, and saw the land, they discouraged the heart of the children of Israel, that they should not go into the land which the Lord had given them.
kjv.NUM_32_10: And the Lord'S anger was kindled the same time, and he sware, saying,
kjv.NUM_32_11: Surely none of the men that came up out of Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see the land which I sware unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob; because they have not wholly followed me:
kjv.NUM_32_12: Save Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenezite, and Joshua the son of Nun: for they have wholly followed the Lord.
kjv.NUM_32_13: And the Lord'S anger was kindled against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until all the generation, that had done evil in the sight of the Lord, was consumed.
kjv.NUM_32_14: And, behold, ye are risen up in your fathers' stead, an increase of sinful men, to augment yet the fierce anger of the Lord toward Israel.
kjv.NUM_32_15: For if ye turn away from after him, he will yet again leave them in the wilderness; and ye shall destroy all this people.
kjv.NUM_32_16: And they came near unto him, and said, We will build sheepfolds here for our cattle, and cities for our little ones:
kjv.NUM_32_17: But we ourselves will go ready armed before the children of Israel, until we have brought them unto their place: and our little ones shall dwell in the fenced cities because of the inhabitants of the land.
kjv.NUM_32_18: We will not return unto our houses, until the children of Israel have inherited every man his inheritance.
kjv.NUM_32_19: For we will not inherit with them on yonder side Jordan, or forward; because our inheritance is fallen to us on this side Jordan eastward.
kjv.NUM_32_20: And Moses said unto them, If ye will do this thing, if ye will go armed before the Lord to war,
kjv.NUM_32_21: And will go all of you armed over Jordan before the Lord, until he hath driven out his enemies from before him,
kjv.NUM_32_22: And the land be subdued before the Lord: then afterward ye shall return, and be guiltless before the Lord, and before Israel; and this land shall be your possession before the Lord.
kjv.NUM_32_23: But if ye will not do so, behold, ye have sinned against the Lord: and be sure your sin will find you out.
kjv.NUM_32_24: Build you cities for your little ones, and folds for your sheep; and do that which hath proceeded out of your mouth.
kjv.NUM_32_25: And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben spake unto Moses, saying, Thy servants will do as my lord commandeth.
kjv.NUM_32_26: Our little ones, our wives, our flocks, and all our cattle, shall be there in the cities of Gilead:
kjv.NUM_32_27: But thy servants will pass over, every man armed for war, before the Lord to battle, as my lord saith.
kjv.NUM_32_28: So concerning them Moses commanded Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the chief fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel:
kjv.NUM_32_29: And Moses said unto them, If the children of Gad and the children of Reuben will pass with you over Jordan, every man armed to battle, before the Lord, and the land shall be subdued before you; then ye shall give them the land of Gilead for a possession:
kjv.NUM_32_30: But if they will not pass over with you armed, they shall have possessions among you in the land of Canaan.
kjv.NUM_32_31: And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben answered, saying, As the Lord hath said unto thy servants, so will we do.
kjv.NUM_32_32: We will pass over armed before the Lord into the land of Canaan, that the possession of our inheritance on this side Jordan may be our's.
kjv.NUM_32_33: And Moses gave unto them, even to the children of Gad, and to the children of Reuben, and unto half the tribe of Manasseh the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, the land, with the cities thereof in the coasts, even the cities of the country round about.
kjv.NUM_32_34: And the children of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,
kjv.NUM_32_35: And Atroth, Shophan, and Jaazer, and Jogbehah,
kjv.NUM_32_36: And Bethnimrah, and Bethharan, fenced cities: and folds for sheep.
kjv.NUM_32_37: And the children of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Kirjathaim,
kjv.NUM_32_38: And Nebo, and Baalmeon, (their names being changed,) and Shibmah: and gave other names unto the cities which they builded.
kjv.NUM_32_39: And the children of Machir the son of Manasseh went to Gilead, and took it, and dispossessed the Amorite which was in it.
kjv.NUM_32_40: And Moses gave Gilead unto Machir the son of Manasseh; and he dwelt therein.
kjv.NUM_32_41: And Jair the son of Manasseh went and took the small towns thereof, and called them Havothjair.
kjv.NUM_32_42: And Nobah went and took Kenath, and the villages thereof, and called it Nobah, after his own name.
kjv.NUM_33_1: These are the journeys of the children of Israel, which went forth out of the land of Egypt with their armies under the hand of Moses and Aaron.
kjv.NUM_33_2: And Moses wrote their goings out according to their journeys by the commandment of the Lord: and these are their journeys according to their goings out.
kjv.NUM_33_3: And they departed from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the morrow after the passover the children of Israel went out with an high hand in the sight of all the Egyptians.
kjv.NUM_33_4: For the Egyptians buried all their firstborn, which the Lord had smitten among them: upon their gods also the Lord executed judgments.
kjv.NUM_33_5: And the children of Israel removed from Rameses, and pitched in Succoth.
kjv.NUM_33_6: And they departed from Succoth, and pitched in Etham, which is in the edge of the wilderness.
kjv.NUM_33_7: And they removed from Etham, and turned again unto Pihahiroth, which is before Baalzephon: and they pitched before Migdol.
kjv.NUM_33_8: And they departed from before Pihahiroth, and passed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness, and went three days'journey in the wilderness of Etham, and pitched in Marah.
kjv.NUM_33_9: And they removed from Marah, and came unto Elim: and in Elim were twelve fountains of water, and threescore and ten palm trees; and they pitched there.
kjv.NUM_33_10: And they removed from Elim, and encamped by the Red sea.
kjv.NUM_33_11: And they removed from the Red sea, and encamped in the wilderness of Sin.
kjv.NUM_33_12: And they took their journey out of the wilderness of Sin, and encamped in Dophkah.
kjv.NUM_33_13: And they departed from Dophkah, and encamped in Alush.
kjv.NUM_33_14: And they removed from Alush, and encamped at Rephidim, where was no water for the people to drink.
kjv.NUM_33_15: And they departed from Rephidim, and pitched in the wilderness of Sinai.
kjv.NUM_33_16: And they removed from the desert of Sinai, and pitched at Kibrothhattaavah.
kjv.NUM_33_17: And they departed from Kibrothhattaavah, and encamped at Hazeroth.
kjv.NUM_33_18: And they departed from Hazeroth, and pitched in Rithmah.
kjv.NUM_33_19: And they departed from Rithmah, and pitched at Rimmonparez.
kjv.NUM_33_20: And they departed from Rimmonparez, and pitched in Libnah.
kjv.NUM_33_21: And they removed from Libnah, and pitched at Rissah.
kjv.NUM_33_22: And they journeyed from Rissah, and pitched in Kehelathah.
kjv.NUM_33_23: And they went from Kehelathah, and pitched in mount Shapher.
kjv.NUM_33_24: And they removed from mount Shapher, and encamped in Haradah.
kjv.NUM_33_25: And they removed from Haradah, and pitched in Makheloth.
kjv.NUM_33_26: And they removed from Makheloth, and encamped at Tahath.
kjv.NUM_33_27: And they departed from Tahath, and pitched at Tarah.
kjv.NUM_33_28: And they removed from Tarah, and pitched in Mithcah.
kjv.NUM_33_29: And they went from Mithcah, and pitched in Hashmonah.
kjv.NUM_33_30: And they departed from Hashmonah, and encamped at Moseroth.
kjv.NUM_33_31: And they departed from Moseroth, and pitched in Benejaakan.
kjv.NUM_33_32: And they removed from Benejaakan, and encamped at Horhagidgad.
kjv.NUM_33_33: And they went from Horhagidgad, and pitched in Jotbathah.
kjv.NUM_33_34: And they removed from Jotbathah, and encamped at Ebronah.
kjv.NUM_33_35: And they departed from Ebronah, and encamped at Eziongaber.
kjv.NUM_33_36: And they removed from Eziongaber, and pitched in the wilderness of Zin, which is Kadesh.
kjv.NUM_33_37: And they removed from Kadesh, and pitched in mount Hor, in the edge of the land of Edom.
kjv.NUM_33_38: And Aaron the priest went up into mount Hor at the commandment of the Lord, and died there, in the fortieth year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt, in the first day of the fifth month.
kjv.NUM_33_39: And Aaron was an hundred and twenty and three years old when he died in mount Hor.
kjv.NUM_33_40: And king Arad the Canaanite, which dwelt in the south in the land of Canaan, heard of the coming of the children of Israel.
kjv.NUM_33_41: And they departed from mount Hor, and pitched in Zalmonah.
kjv.NUM_33_42: And they departed from Zalmonah, and pitched in Punon.
kjv.NUM_33_43: And they departed from Punon, and pitched in Oboth.
kjv.NUM_33_44: And they departed from Oboth, and pitched in Ijeabarim, in the border of Moab.
kjv.NUM_33_45: And they departed from Iim, and pitched in Dibongad.
kjv.NUM_33_46: And they removed from Dibongad, and encamped in Almondiblathaim.
kjv.NUM_33_47: And they removed from Almondiblathaim, and pitched in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo.
kjv.NUM_33_48: And they departed from the mountains of Abarim, and pitched in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.
kjv.NUM_33_49: And they pitched by Jordan, from Bethjesimoth even unto Abelshittim in the plains of Moab.
kjv.NUM_33_50: And the Lord spake unto Moses in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying,
kjv.NUM_33_51: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye are passed over Jordan into the land of Canaan;
kjv.NUM_33_52: Then ye shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and destroy all their pictures, and destroy all their molten images, and quite pluck down all their high places:
kjv.NUM_33_53: And ye shall dispossess the inhabitants of the land, and dwell therein: for I have given you the land to possess it.
kjv.NUM_33_54: And ye shall divide the land by lot for an inheritance among your families: and to the more ye shall give the more inheritance, and to the fewer ye shall give the less inheritance: every man's inheritance shall be in the place where his lot falleth; according to the tribes of your fathers ye shall inherit.
kjv.NUM_33_55: But if ye will not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you; then it shall come to pass, that those which ye let remain of them shall be pricks in your eyes, and thorns in your sides, and shall vex you in the land wherein ye dwell.
kjv.NUM_33_56: Moreover it shall come to pass, that I shall do unto you, as I thought to do unto them.
kjv.NUM_34_1: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
kjv.NUM_34_2: Command the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land of Canaan; (this is the land that shall fall unto you for an inheritance, even the land of Canaan with the coasts thereof:)
kjv.NUM_34_3: Then your south quarter shall be from the wilderness of Zin along by the coast of Edom, and your south border shall be the outmost coast of the salt sea eastward:
kjv.NUM_34_4: And your border shall turn from the south to the ascent of Akrabbim, and pass on to Zin: and the going forth thereof shall be from the south to Kadeshbarnea, and shall go on to Hazaraddar, and pass on to Azmon:
kjv.NUM_34_5: And the border shall fetch a compass from Azmon unto the river of Egypt, and the goings out of it shall be at the sea.
kjv.NUM_34_6: And as for the western border, ye shall even have the great sea for a border: this shall be your west border.
kjv.NUM_34_7: And this shall be your north border: from the great sea ye shall point out for you mount Hor:
kjv.NUM_34_8: From mount Hor ye shall point out your border unto the entrance of Hamath; and the goings forth of the border shall be to Zedad:
kjv.NUM_34_9: And the border shall go on to Ziphron, and the goings out of it shall be at Hazarenan: this shall be your north border.
kjv.NUM_34_10: And ye shall point out your east border from Hazarenan to Shepham:
kjv.NUM_34_11: And the coast shall go down from Shepham to Riblah, on the east side of Ain; and the border shall descend, and shall reach unto the side of the sea of Chinnereth eastward:
kjv.NUM_34_12: And the border shall go down to Jordan, and the goings out of it shall be at the salt sea: this shall be your land with the coasts thereof round about.
kjv.NUM_34_13: And Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying, This is the land which ye shall inherit by lot, which the Lord commanded to give unto the nine tribes, and to the half tribe:
kjv.NUM_34_14: For the tribe of the children of Reuben according to the house of their fathers, and the tribe of the children of Gad according to the house of their fathers, have received their inheritance; and half the tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance:
kjv.NUM_34_15: The two tribes and the half tribe have received their inheritance on this side Jordan near Jericho eastward, toward the sunrising.
kjv.NUM_34_16: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
kjv.NUM_34_17: These are the names of the men which shall divide the land unto you: Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun.
kjv.NUM_34_18: And ye shall take one prince of every tribe, to divide the land by inheritance.
kjv.NUM_34_19: And the names of the men are these: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.
kjv.NUM_34_20: And of the tribe of the children of Simeon, Shemuel the son of Ammihud.
kjv.NUM_34_21: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad the son of Chislon.
kjv.NUM_34_22: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Dan, Bukki the son of Jogli.
kjv.NUM_34_23: The prince of the children of Joseph, for the tribe of the children of Manasseh, Hanniel the son of Ephod.
kjv.NUM_34_24: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Ephraim, Kemuel the son of Shiphtan.
kjv.NUM_34_25: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Zebulun, Elizaphan the son of Parnach.
kjv.NUM_34_26: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Issachar, Paltiel the son of Azzan.
kjv.NUM_34_27: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Asher, Ahihud the son of Shelomi.
kjv.NUM_34_28: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Naphtali, Pedahel the son of Ammihud.
kjv.NUM_34_29: These are they whom the Lord commanded to divide the inheritance unto the children of Israel in the land of Canaan.
kjv.NUM_35_1: And the Lord spake unto Moses in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying,
kjv.NUM_35_2: Command the children of Israel, that they give unto the Levites of the inheritance of their possession cities to dwell in; and ye shall give also unto the Levites suburbs for the cities round about them.
kjv.NUM_35_3: And the cities shall they have to dwell in; and the suburbs of them shall be for their cattle, and for their goods, and for all their beasts.
kjv.NUM_35_4: And the suburbs of the cities, which ye shall give unto the Levites, shall reach from the wall of the city and outward a thousand cubits round about.
kjv.NUM_35_5: And ye shall measure from without the city on the east side two thousand cubits, and on the south side two thousand cubits, and on the west side two thousand cubits, and on the north side two thousand cubits; and the city shall be in the midst: this shall be to them the suburbs of the cities.
kjv.NUM_35_6: And among the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites there shall be six cities for refuge, which ye shall appoint for the manslayer, that he may flee thither: and to them ye shall add forty and two cities.
kjv.NUM_35_7: So all the cities which ye shall give to the Levites shall be forty and eight cities: them shall ye give with their suburbs.
kjv.NUM_35_8: And the cities which ye shall give shall be of the possession of the children of Israel: from them that have many ye shall give many; but from them that have few ye shall give few: every one shall give of his cities unto the Levites according to his inheritance which he inheriteth.
kjv.NUM_35_9: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
kjv.NUM_35_10: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come over Jordan into the land of Canaan;
kjv.NUM_35_11: Then ye shall appoint you cities to be cities of refuge for you; that the slayer may flee thither, which killeth any person at unawares.
kjv.NUM_35_12: And they shall be unto you cities for refuge from the avenger; that the manslayer die not, until he stand before the congregation in judgment.
kjv.NUM_35_13: And of these cities which ye shall give six cities shall ye have for refuge.
kjv.NUM_35_14: Ye shall give three cities on this side Jordan, and three cities shall ye give in the land of Canaan, which shall be cities of refuge.
kjv.NUM_35_15: These six cities shall be a refuge, both for the children of Israel, and for the stranger, and for the sojourner among them: that every one that killeth any person unawares may flee thither.
kjv.NUM_35_16: And if he smite him with an instrument of iron, so that he die, he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
kjv.NUM_35_17: And if he smite him with throwing a stone, wherewith he may die, and he die, he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
kjv.NUM_35_18: Or if he smite him with an hand weapon of wood, wherewith he may die, and he die, he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
kjv.NUM_35_19: The revenger of blood himself shall slay the murderer: when he meeteth him, he shall slay him.
kjv.NUM_35_20: But if he thrust him of hatred, or hurl at him by laying of wait, that he die;
kjv.NUM_35_21: Or in enmity smite him with his hand, that he die: he that smote him shall surely be put to death; for he is a murderer: the revenger of blood shall slay the murderer, when he meeteth him.
kjv.NUM_35_22: But if he thrust him suddenly without enmity, or have cast upon him any thing without laying of wait,
kjv.NUM_35_23: Or with any stone, wherewith a man may die, seeing him not, and cast it upon him, that he die, and was not his enemy, neither sought his harm:
kjv.NUM_35_24: Then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the revenger of blood according to these judgments:
kjv.NUM_35_25: And the congregation shall deliver the slayer out of the hand of the revenger of blood, and the congregation shall restore him to the city of his refuge, whither he was fled: and he shall abide in it unto the death of the high priest, which was anointed with the holy oil.
kjv.NUM_35_26: But if the slayer shall at any time come without the border of the city of his refuge, whither he was fled;
kjv.NUM_35_27: And the revenger of blood find him without the borders of the city of his refuge, and the revenger of blood kill the slayer; he shall not be guilty of blood:
kjv.NUM_35_28: Because he should have remained in the city of his refuge until the death of the high priest: but after the death of the high priest the slayer shall return into the land of his possession.
kjv.NUM_35_29: So these things shall be for a statute of judgment unto you throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
kjv.NUM_35_30: Whoso killeth any person, the murderer shall be put to death by the mouth of witnesses: but one witness shall not testify against any person to cause him to die.
kjv.NUM_35_31: Moreover ye shall take no satisfaction for the life of a murderer, which is guilty of death: but he shall be surely put to death.
kjv.NUM_35_32: And ye shall take no satisfaction for him that is fled to the city of his refuge, that he should come again to dwell in the land, until the death of the priest.
kjv.NUM_35_33: So ye shall not pollute the land wherein ye are: for blood it defileth the land: and the land cannot be cleansed of the blood that is shed therein, but by the blood of him that shed it.
kjv.NUM_35_34: Defile not therefore the land which ye shall inhabit, wherein I dwell: for I the Lord dwell among the children of Israel.
kjv.NUM_36_1: And the chief fathers of the families of the children of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, of the families of the sons of Joseph, came near, and spake before Moses, and before the princes, the chief fathers of the children of Israel:
kjv.NUM_36_2: And they said, The Lord commanded my lord to give the land for an inheritance by lot to the children of Israel: and my lord was commanded by the Lord to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother unto his daughters.
kjv.NUM_36_3: And if they be married to any of the sons of the other tribes of the children of Israel, then shall their inheritance be taken from the inheritance of our fathers, and shall be put to the inheritance of the tribe whereunto they are received: so shall it be taken from the lot of our inheritance.
kjv.NUM_36_4: And when the jubile of the children of Israel shall be, then shall their inheritance be put unto the inheritance of the tribe whereunto they are received: so shall their inheritance be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers.
kjv.NUM_36_5: And Moses commanded the children of Israel according to the word of the Lord, saying, The tribe of the sons of Joseph hath said well.
kjv.NUM_36_6: This is the thing which the Lord doth command concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying, Let them marry to whom they think best; only to the family of the tribe of their father shall they marry.
kjv.NUM_36_7: So shall not the inheritance of the children of Israel remove from tribe to tribe: for every one of the children of Israel shall keep himself to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers.
kjv.NUM_36_8: And every daughter, that possesseth an inheritance in any tribe of the children of Israel, shall be wife unto one of the family of the tribe of her father, that the children of Israel may enjoy every man the inheritance of his fathers.
kjv.NUM_36_9: Neither shall the inheritance remove from one tribe to another tribe; but every one of the tribes of the children of Israel shall keep himself to his own inheritance.
kjv.NUM_36_10: Even as the Lord commanded Moses, so did the daughters of Zelophehad:
kjv.NUM_36_11: For Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, were married unto their fathers brothers' sons:
kjv.NUM_36_12: And they were married into the families of the sons of Manasseh the son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained in the tribe of the family of their father.
kjv.NUM_36_13: These are the commandments and the judgments, which the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses unto the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.

net.NUM_1_1: Now the Lord spoke to Moses in the tent of meeting in the desert of Sinai on the first day of the second month of the second year after the Israelites departed from the land of Egypt. He said:
net.NUM_1_2: “Take a census of the entire Israelite community by their clans and families, counting the name of every individual male.
net.NUM_1_3: You and Aaron are to number all in Israel who can serve in the army, those who are twenty years old or older, by their divisions.
net.NUM_1_4: And to help you there is to be a man from each tribe, each man the head of his family.
net.NUM_1_5: Now these are the names of the men who are to help you: from Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;
net.NUM_1_6: from Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
net.NUM_1_7: from Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab;
net.NUM_1_8: from Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar;
net.NUM_1_9: from Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon;
net.NUM_1_10: from the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; from Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;
net.NUM_1_11: from Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;
net.NUM_1_12: from Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
net.NUM_1_13: from Asher, Pagiel son of Ocran;
net.NUM_1_14: from Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;
net.NUM_1_15: from Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.” The Census of the Tribes
net.NUM_1_16: These were the ones chosen from the community, leaders of their ancestral tribes. They were the heads of the thousands of Israel.
net.NUM_1_17: So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been mentioned specifically by name,
net.NUM_1_18: and they assembled the entire community together on the first day of the second month. Then the people recorded their ancestry by their clans and families, and the men who were twenty years old or older were listed by name individually,
net.NUM_1_19: just as the Lord had commanded Moses. And so he numbered them in the desert of Sinai.
net.NUM_1_20: And they were as follows: The descendants of Reuben, the firstborn son of Israel: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name individually.
net.NUM_1_21: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Reuben were 46,500.
net.NUM_1_22: From the descendants of Simeon: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males numbered of them twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name individually.
net.NUM_1_23: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Simeon were 59,300.
net.NUM_1_24: From the descendants of Gad: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_25: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Gad were 45,650.
net.NUM_1_26: From the descendants of Judah: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_27: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Judah were 74,600.
net.NUM_1_28: From the descendants of Issachar: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_29: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Issachar were 54,400.
net.NUM_1_30: From the descendants of Zebulun: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_31: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Zebulun were 57,400.
net.NUM_1_32: From the sons of Joseph: From the descendants of Ephraim: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_33: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Ephraim were 40,500.
net.NUM_1_34: From the descendants of Manasseh: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_35: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Manasseh were 32,200.
net.NUM_1_36: From the descendants of Benjamin: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_37: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Benjamin were 35,400.
net.NUM_1_38: From the descendants of Dan: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_39: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Dan were 62,700.
net.NUM_1_40: From the descendants of Asher: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_41: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Asher were 41,500.
net.NUM_1_42: From the descendants of Naphtali: According to the records of their clans and families, all the males twenty years old or older who could serve in the army were listed by name.
net.NUM_1_43: Those of them who were numbered from the tribe of Naphtali were 53,400.
net.NUM_1_44: These were the men whom Moses and Aaron numbered along with the twelve leaders of Israel, each of whom was from his own family.
net.NUM_1_45: All the Israelites who were twenty years old or older, who could serve in Israel’s army, were numbered according to their families.
net.NUM_1_46: And all those numbered totaled 603,550. The Exemption of the Levites
net.NUM_1_47: But the Levites, according to the tribe of their fathers, were not numbered among them.
net.NUM_1_48: The Lord had said to Moses,
net.NUM_1_49: “Only the tribe of Levi you must not number or count with the other Israelites.
net.NUM_1_50: But appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony, over all its furnishings and over everything in it. They must carry the tabernacle and all its furnishings; and they must attend to it and camp around it.
net.NUM_1_51: Whenever the tabernacle is to move, the Levites must take it down, and whenever the tabernacle is to be reassembled, the Levites must set it up. Any unauthorized person who approaches it must be killed.
net.NUM_1_52: “The Israelites will camp according to their divisions, each man in his camp, and each man by his standard.
net.NUM_1_53: But the Levites must camp around the tabernacle of the testimony, so that the Lord’s anger will not fall on the Israelite community. The Levites are responsible for the care of the tabernacle of the testimony.”
net.NUM_1_54: The Israelites did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses —that is what they did.
net.NUM_2_1: The Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron:
net.NUM_2_2: “Every one of the Israelites must camp under his standard with the emblems of his family; they must camp at some distance around the tent of meeting. The Tribes on the East
net.NUM_2_3: “Now those who will be camping on the east, toward the sunrise, are the divisions of the camp of Judah under their standard. The leader of the people of Judah is Nahshon son of Amminadab.
net.NUM_2_4: Those numbered in his division are 74,600.
net.NUM_2_5: Those who will be camping next to them are the tribe of Issachar. The leader of the people of Issachar is Nethanel son of Zuar.
net.NUM_2_6: Those numbered in his division are 54,400.
net.NUM_2_7: Next will be the tribe of Zebulun. The leader of the people of Zebulun is Eliab son of Helon.
net.NUM_2_8: Those numbered in his division are 57,400.
net.NUM_2_9: All those numbered of the camp of Judah, according to their divisions, are 186,400. They will travel at the front. The Tribes on the South
net.NUM_2_10: “On the south will be the divisions of the camp of Reuben under their standard. The leader of the people of Reuben is Elizur son of Shedeur.
net.NUM_2_11: Those numbered in his division are 46,500.
net.NUM_2_12: Those who will be camping next to them are the tribe of Simeon. The leader of the people of Simeon is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
net.NUM_2_13: Those numbered in his division are 59,300.
net.NUM_2_14: Next will be the tribe of Gad. The leader of the people of Gad is Eliasaph son of Deuel.
net.NUM_2_15: Those numbered in his division are 45,650.
net.NUM_2_16: All those numbered of the camp of Reuben, according to their divisions, are 151,450. They will travel second. The Tribe in the Center
net.NUM_2_17: “Then the tent of meeting with the camp of the Levites will travel in the middle of the camps. They will travel in the same order as they camped, each in his own place under his standard. The Tribes on the West
net.NUM_2_18: “On the west will be the divisions of the camp of Ephraim under their standard. The leader of the people of Ephraim is Elishama son of Ammihud.
net.NUM_2_19: Those numbered in his division are 40,500.
net.NUM_2_20: Next to them will be the tribe of Manasseh. The leader of the people of Manasseh is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
net.NUM_2_21: Those numbered in his division are 32,200.
net.NUM_2_22: Next will be the tribe of Benjamin. The leader of the people of Benjamin is Abidan son of Gideoni.
net.NUM_2_23: Those numbered in his division are 35,400.
net.NUM_2_24: All those numbered of the camp of Ephraim, according to their divisions, are 108,100. They will travel third. The Tribes on the North
net.NUM_2_25: “On the north will be the divisions of the camp of Dan, under their standards. The leader of the people of Dan is Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
net.NUM_2_26: Those numbered in his division are 62,700.
net.NUM_2_27: Those who will be camping next to them are the tribe of Asher. The leader of the people of Asher is Pagiel son of Ocran.
net.NUM_2_28: Those numbered in his division are 41,500.
net.NUM_2_29: Next will be the tribe of Naphtali. The leader of the people of Naphtali is Ahira son of Enan.
net.NUM_2_30: Those numbered in his division are 53,400.
net.NUM_2_31: All those numbered of the camp of Dan are 157,600. They will travel last, under their standards.” Summary
net.NUM_2_32: These are the Israelites, numbered according to their families. All those numbered in the camps, by their divisions, are 603,550.
net.NUM_2_33: But the Levites were not numbered among the other Israelites, as the Lord commanded Moses.
net.NUM_2_34: So the Israelites did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses; that is the way they camped under their standards, and that is the way they traveled, each with his clan and family.
net.NUM_3_1: Now these are the records of Aaron and Moses when the Lord spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
net.NUM_3_2: These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
net.NUM_3_3: These are the names of the sons of Aaron, the anointed priests, whom he consecrated to minister as priests.
net.NUM_3_4: Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord when they offered strange fire before the Lord in the desert of Sinai, and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar ministered as priests in the presence of Aaron their father. The Assignment of the Levites
net.NUM_3_6: “Bring the tribe of Levi near, and present them before Aaron the priest, that they may serve him.
net.NUM_3_7: They are responsible for his needs and the needs of the whole community before the tent of meeting, by attending to the service of the tabernacle.
net.NUM_3_8: And they are responsible for all the furnishings of the tent of meeting, and for the needs of the Israelites, as they serve in the tabernacle.
net.NUM_3_9: You are to assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they will be assigned exclusively to him out of all the Israelites.
net.NUM_3_10: So you are to appoint Aaron and his sons, and they will be responsible for their priesthood, but the unauthorized person who comes near must be put to death.”
net.NUM_3_11: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.NUM_3_12: “Look, I myself have taken the Levites from among the Israelites instead of every firstborn who opens the womb among the Israelites. So the Levites belong to me,
net.NUM_3_13: because all the firstborn are mine. When I destroyed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I set apart for myself all the firstborn in Israel, both man and beast. They belong to me. I am the Lord.” The Numbering of the Levites
net.NUM_3_14: Then the Lord spoke to Moses in the desert of Sinai:
net.NUM_3_15: “Number the Levites by their clans and their families; every male from a month old and upward you are to number.”
net.NUM_3_16: So Moses numbered them according to the word of the Lord, just as he had been commanded. The Summary of Families
net.NUM_3_17: These were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
net.NUM_3_18: These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei.
net.NUM_3_19: The sons of Kohath by their families were: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
net.NUM_3_20: The sons of Merari by their families were Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their clans. The Numbering of the Gershonites
net.NUM_3_21: From Gershon came the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimeites; these were the families of the Gershonites.
net.NUM_3_22: Those of them who were numbered, counting every male from a month old and upward, were 7,500.
net.NUM_3_23: The families of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle toward the west.
net.NUM_3_24: Now the leader of the clan of the Gershonites was Eliasaph son of Lael.
net.NUM_3_25: And the responsibilities of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting included the tabernacle, the tent with its covering, the curtain at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
net.NUM_3_26: the hangings of the courtyard, the curtain at the entrance to the courtyard that surrounded the tabernacle and the altar, and their ropes, plus all the service connected with these things. The Numbering of the Kohathites
net.NUM_3_27: From Kohath came the family of the Amramites, the family of the Izharites, the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites; these were the families of the Kohathites.
net.NUM_3_28: Counting every male from a month old and upward, there were 8,600. They were responsible for the care of the sanctuary.
net.NUM_3_29: The families of the Kohathites were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle.
net.NUM_3_30: Now the leader of the clan of the families of the Kohathites was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
net.NUM_3_31: Their responsibilities included the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars, and the utensils of the sanctuary with which they ministered, the curtain, and all their service.
net.NUM_3_32: Now the head of all the Levitical leaders was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest. He was appointed over those who were responsible for the sanctuary. The Numbering of Merari
net.NUM_3_33: From Merari came the family of the Mahlites and the family of the Mushites; these were the families of Merari.
net.NUM_3_34: Those of them who were numbered, counting every male from a month old and upward, were 6,200.
net.NUM_3_35: Now the leader of the clan of the families of Merari was Zuriel son of Abihail. These were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
net.NUM_3_36: The appointed responsibilities of the Merarites included the frames of the tabernacle, its crossbars, its posts, its sockets, its utensils, plus all the service connected with these things,
net.NUM_3_37: and the pillars of the courtyard all around, with their sockets, their pegs, and their ropes.
net.NUM_3_38: But those who were to camp in front of the tabernacle on the east, in front of the tent of meeting, were Moses, Aaron, and his sons. They were responsible for the needs of the sanctuary and for the needs of the Israelites, but the unauthorized person who approached was to be put to death.
net.NUM_3_39: All who were numbered of the Levites, whom Moses and Aaron numbered by the word of the Lord, according to their families, every male from a month old and upward, were 22,000. The Substitution for the Firstborn
net.NUM_3_40: Then the Lord said to Moses, “Number all the firstborn males of the Israelites from a month old and upward, and take the number of their names.
net.NUM_3_41: And take the Levites for me—I am the Lord—instead of all the firstborn males among the Israelites, and the livestock of the Levites instead of all the firstborn of the livestock of the Israelites.”
net.NUM_3_42: So Moses numbered all the firstborn males among the Israelites, as the Lord had commanded him.
net.NUM_3_43: And all the firstborn males, by the number of the names from a month old and upward, totaled 22,273.
net.NUM_3_44: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.NUM_3_45: “Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn males among the Israelites, and the livestock of the Levites instead of their livestock. And the Levites will be mine. I am the Lord.
net.NUM_3_46: And for the redemption of the 273 firstborn males of the Israelites who exceed the number of the Levites,
net.NUM_3_47: collect five shekels for each one individually; you are to collect this amount in the currency of the sanctuary shekel (this shekel is twenty gerahs).
net.NUM_3_48: And give the money for the redemption of the excess number of them to Aaron and his sons.”
net.NUM_3_49: So Moses took the redemption money from those who were in excess of those redeemed by the Levites.
net.NUM_3_50: From the firstborn males of the Israelites he collected the money, 1,365 shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
net.NUM_3_51: Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons, according to the word of the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.NUM_4_1: Then the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron:
net.NUM_4_2: “Take a census of the Kohathites from among the Levites, by their families and by their clans,
net.NUM_4_3: from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, all who enter the company to do the work in the tent of meeting.
net.NUM_4_4: This is the service of the Kohathites in the tent of meeting, relating to the most holy things.
net.NUM_4_5: When it is time for the camp to journey, Aaron and his sons must come and take down the screening curtain and cover the ark of the testimony with it.
net.NUM_4_6: Then they must put over it a covering of fine leather and spread over that a cloth entirely of blue, and then they must insert its poles.
net.NUM_4_7: “On the table of the presence they must spread a blue cloth, and put on it the dishes, the pans, the bowls, and the pitchers for pouring, and the Bread of the Presence must be on it continually.
net.NUM_4_8: They must spread over them a scarlet cloth, and cover the same with a covering of fine leather; and they must insert its poles.
net.NUM_4_9: “They must take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand of the light, with its lamps, its wick-trimmers, its trays, and all its oil vessels, with which they service it.
net.NUM_4_10: Then they must put it with all its utensils in a covering of fine leather, and put it on a carrying beam.
net.NUM_4_11: “They must spread a blue cloth on the gold altar, and cover it with a covering of fine leather; and they must insert its poles.
net.NUM_4_12: Then they must take all the utensils of the service, with which they serve in the sanctuary, put them in a blue cloth, cover them with a covering of fine leather, and put them on a carrying beam.
net.NUM_4_13: Also, they must take away the ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it.
net.NUM_4_14: Then they must place on it all its implements with which they serve there—the trays, the meat forks, the shovels, the basins, and all the utensils of the altar—and they must spread on it a covering of fine leather, and then insert its poles.
net.NUM_4_15: “When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary, when the camp is ready to journey, then the Kohathites will come to carry them; but they must not touch any holy thing, or they will die. These are the responsibilities of the Kohathites with the tent of meeting.
net.NUM_4_16: “The appointed responsibility of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest is for the oil for the light, and the spiced incense, and the daily grain offering, and the anointing oil; he also has the appointed responsibility over all the tabernacle with all that is in it, over the sanctuary and over all its furnishings.”
net.NUM_4_17: Then the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron:
net.NUM_4_18: “Do not allow the tribe of the families of the Kohathites to be cut off from among the Levites;
net.NUM_4_19: but in order that they will live and not die when they approach the most holy things, do this for them: Aaron and his sons will go in and appoint each man to his service and his responsibility.
net.NUM_4_20: But the Kohathites are not to go in to watch while the holy things are being covered, or they will die.” The Service of the Gershonites
net.NUM_4_21: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.NUM_4_22: “Also take a census of the Gershonites also, by their clans and by their families.
net.NUM_4_23: You must number them from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, all who enter the company to do the work of the tent of meeting.
net.NUM_4_24: This is the service of the families of Gershonites, as they serve and carry it.
net.NUM_4_25: They must carry the curtains for the tabernacle and the tent of meeting with its covering, the covering of fine leather that is over it, the curtains for the entrance of the tent of meeting,
net.NUM_4_26: the hangings for the courtyard, the curtain for the entrance of the gate of the court, which is around the tabernacle and the altar, and their ropes, along with all the furnishings for their service and everything that is made for them. So they are to serve.
net.NUM_4_27: “All the service of the Gershonites, whether carrying loads or for any of their work, will be at the direction of Aaron and his sons. You will assign them all their tasks as their responsibility.
net.NUM_4_28: This is the service of the families of the Gershonites concerning the tent of meeting. Their responsibilities will be under the authority of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest. The Service of the Merarites
net.NUM_4_29: “As for the sons of Merari, you are to number them by their families and by their clans.
net.NUM_4_30: You must number them from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, all who enter the company to do the work of the tent of meeting.
net.NUM_4_31: This is what they are responsible to carry as their entire service in the tent of meeting: the frames of the tabernacle, its crossbars, its posts, its sockets,
net.NUM_4_32: and the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their sockets, tent pegs, and ropes, along with all their furnishings and everything for their service. You are to assign by name the items that each man is responsible to carry.
net.NUM_4_33: This is the service of the families of the Merarites, their entire service concerning the tent of meeting, under the authority of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.” Summary
net.NUM_4_34: So Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the community numbered the Kohathites by their families and by clans,
net.NUM_4_35: from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, everyone who entered the company for the work in the tent of meeting;
net.NUM_4_36: and those of them numbered by their families were 2,750.
net.NUM_4_37: These were those numbered from the families of the Kohathites, everyone who served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the word of the Lord by the authority of Moses.
net.NUM_4_38: Those numbered from the Gershonites, by their families and by their clans,
net.NUM_4_39: from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, everyone who entered the company for the work in the tent of meeting—
net.NUM_4_40: those of them numbered by their families, by their clans, were 2,630.
net.NUM_4_41: These were those numbered from the families of the Gershonites, everyone who served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the word of the Lord.
net.NUM_4_42: Those numbered from the families of the Merarites, by their families, by their clans,
net.NUM_4_43: from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, everyone who entered the company for the work in the tent of meeting—
net.NUM_4_44: those of them numbered by their families were 3,200.
net.NUM_4_45: These are those numbered from the families of the Merarites, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the word of the Lord by the authority of Moses.
net.NUM_4_46: All who were numbered of the Levites, whom Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of Israel numbered by their families and by their clans,
net.NUM_4_47: from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, everyone who entered to do the work of service and the work of carrying relating to the tent of meeting—
net.NUM_4_49: According to the word of the Lord they were numbered, by the authority of Moses, each according to his service and according to what he was to carry. Thus were they numbered by him, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.NUM_5_1: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.NUM_5_2: “Command the Israelites to expel from the camp every leper, everyone who has a discharge, and whoever becomes defiled by a corpse.
net.NUM_5_3: You must expel both men and women; you must put them outside the camp, so that they will not defile their camps, among which I live.”
net.NUM_5_4: So the Israelites did so, and expelled them outside the camp. As the Lord had spoken to Moses, so the Israelites did. Restitution for Sin
net.NUM_5_5: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.NUM_5_6: “Tell the Israelites, ‘When a man or a woman commits any sin that people commit, thereby breaking faith with the Lord, and that person is found guilty,
net.NUM_5_7: then he must confess his sin that he has committed and must make full reparation, add one-fifth to it, and give it to whomever he wronged.
net.NUM_5_8: But if the individual has no close relative to whom reparation can be made for the wrong, the reparation for the wrong must be paid to the Lord for the priest, in addition to the ram of atonement by which atonement is made for him.
net.NUM_5_9: Every offering of all the Israelites’ holy things that they bring to the priest will be his.
net.NUM_5_10: Every man’s holy things will be his; whatever any man gives the priest will be his.’” The Jealousy Ordeal
net.NUM_5_12: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them, ‘If any man’s wife goes astray and behaves unfaithfully toward him,
net.NUM_5_13: and a man goes to bed with her for sexual relations without her husband knowing it, and it is undetected that she has defiled herself since there was no witness against her, nor was she caught in the act—
net.NUM_5_14: and if jealous feelings come over him and he becomes suspicious of his wife when she is defiled, or if jealous feelings come over him and he becomes suspicious of his wife, when she is not defiled—
net.NUM_5_15: then the man must bring his wife to the priest, and he must bring the offering required for her, one-tenth of an ephah of barley meal; he must not pour olive oil on it or put frankincense on it because it is a grain offering of suspicion, a grain offering for remembering, for bringing iniquity to remembrance.
net.NUM_5_16: “‘Then the priest will bring her near and have her stand before the Lord.
net.NUM_5_17: The priest will then take holy water in a pottery jar, and take some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle, and put it into the water.
net.NUM_5_18: Then the priest will have the woman stand before the Lord, and he will uncover the woman’s head and put the grain offering for remembering in her hands, which is the grain offering of suspicion. The priest will hold in his hand the bitter water that brings a curse.
net.NUM_5_19: Then the priest will put the woman under oath and say to her, “If no other man has gone to bed with you, and if you have not gone astray and become defiled while under your husband’s authority, may you be free from this bitter water that brings a curse.
net.NUM_5_20: But if you have gone astray while under your husband’s authority, and if you have defiled yourself and some man other than your husband has had sexual relations with you—”
net.NUM_5_21: (then the priest will put the woman under the oath of the curse and will say to her) “the Lord make you an attested curse among your people if the Lord makes your thigh fall away and your abdomen swell,
net.NUM_5_22: and this water that causes the curse will go into your stomach and make your abdomen swell and your thigh rot.” Then the woman must say, “Amen, amen.”
net.NUM_5_23: “‘Then the priest will write these curses on a scroll and then scrape them off into the bitter water.
net.NUM_5_24: He will make the woman drink the bitter water that brings a curse, and the water that brings a curse will enter her to produce bitterness.
net.NUM_5_25: The priest will take the grain offering of suspicion from the woman’s hand, wave the grain offering before the Lord, and bring it to the altar.
net.NUM_5_26: Then the priest will take a handful of the grain offering as its memorial portion, burn it on the altar, and afterward make the woman drink the water.
net.NUM_5_27: When he has made her drink the water, then if she has defiled herself and behaved unfaithfully toward her husband, the water that brings a curse will enter her to produce bitterness—her abdomen will swell, her thigh will fall away, and the woman will become a curse among her people.
net.NUM_5_28: But if the woman has not defiled herself, and is clean, then she will be free of ill effects and will be able to bear children.
net.NUM_5_29: “‘This is the law for cases of jealousy, when a wife, while under her husband’s authority, goes astray and defiles herself,
net.NUM_5_30: or when jealous feelings come over a man and he becomes suspicious of his wife; then he must have the woman stand before the Lord, and the priest will carry out all this law upon her.
net.NUM_5_31: Then the man will be free from iniquity, but that woman will bear the consequences of her iniquity.’”
net.NUM_6_1: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.NUM_6_2: “Speak to the Israelites, and tell them, ‘When someone—either a man or a woman —takes a special vow, to take a vow as a Nazirite, to separate himself to the Lord,
net.NUM_6_3: he must separate himself from wine and strong drink; he must drink neither vinegar made from wine nor vinegar made from strong drink, nor may he drink any juice of grapes, nor eat fresh grapes or raisins.
net.NUM_6_4: All the days of his separation he must not eat anything that is produced by the grapevine, from seed to skin.
net.NUM_6_5: “‘All the days of the vow of his separation no razor may be used on his head until the time is fulfilled for which he separated himself to the Lord. He will be holy, and he must let the locks of hair on his head grow long.
net.NUM_6_6: “‘All the days that he separates himself to the Lord he must not contact a dead body.
net.NUM_6_7: He must not defile himself even for his father or his mother or his brother or his sister if they die, because the separation for his God is on his head.
net.NUM_6_8: All the days of his separation he must be holy to the Lord. Contingencies for Defilement
net.NUM_6_9: “‘If anyone dies very suddenly beside him and he defiles his consecrated head, then he must shave his head on the day of his purification—on the seventh day he must shave it.
net.NUM_6_10: On the eighth day he is to bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest, to the entrance to the tent of meeting.
net.NUM_6_11: Then the priest will offer one for a purification offering and the other as a burnt offering, and make atonement for him, because of his transgression in regard to the corpse. So he must reconsecrate his head on that day.
net.NUM_6_12: He must rededicate to the Lord the days of his separation and bring a male lamb in its first year as a reparation offering, but the former days will not be counted because his separation was defiled. Fulfilling the Vows
net.NUM_6_13: “‘Now this is the law of the Nazirite: When the days of his separation are fulfilled, he must be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting,
net.NUM_6_14: and he must present his offering to the Lord: one male lamb in its first year without blemish for a burnt offering, one ewe lamb in its first year without blemish for a purification offering, one ram without blemish for a peace offering,
net.NUM_6_15: and a basket of bread made without yeast, cakes of fine flour mixed with olive oil, wafers made without yeast and smeared with olive oil, and their grain offering and their drink offerings.
net.NUM_6_16: “‘Then the priest must present all these before the Lord and offer his purification offering and his burnt offering.
net.NUM_6_17: Then he must offer the ram as a peace offering to the Lord along with the basket of bread made without yeast; the priest must also offer his grain offering and his drink offering.
net.NUM_6_18: “‘Then the Nazirite must shave his consecrated head at the entrance to the tent of meeting and must take the hair from his consecrated head and put it on the fire where the peace offering is burning.
net.NUM_6_19: And the priest must take the boiled shoulder of the ram, one cake made without yeast from the basket, and one wafer made without yeast, and put them on the hands of the Nazirite after he has shaved his consecrated head;
net.NUM_6_20: then the priest must wave them as a wave offering before the Lord; it is a holy portion for the priest, together with the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the raised offering. After this the Nazirite may drink wine.
net.NUM_6_21: “‘This is the law of the Nazirite who vows to the Lord his offering according to his separation, as well as whatever else he can provide. Thus he must fulfill his vow that he makes, according to the law of his separation.’” The Priestly Benediction
net.NUM_6_23: “Tell Aaron and his sons, ‘This is the way you are to bless the Israelites. Say to them:
net.NUM_6_24: “The Lord bless you and protect you;
net.NUM_6_25: The Lord make his face to shine upon you, and be gracious to you;
net.NUM_6_26: The Lord lift up his countenance upon you and give you peace.”’
net.NUM_6_27: “So they will put my name on the Israelites, and I will bless them.”
net.NUM_7_1: When Moses had completed setting up the tabernacle, he anointed it and consecrated it and all its furnishings, and he anointed and consecrated the altar and all its utensils.
net.NUM_7_2: Then the leaders of Israel, the heads of their clans, made an offering. They were the leaders of the tribes; they were the ones who had been supervising the numbering.
net.NUM_7_3: They brought their offerings before the Lord, six covered carts and twelve oxen—one cart for every two of the leaders, and an ox for each one; and they presented them in front of the tabernacle. The Distribution of the Gifts
net.NUM_7_4: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.NUM_7_5: “Receive these gifts from them, that they may be used in doing the work of the tent of meeting; and you must give them to the Levites, to every man as his service requires.”
net.NUM_7_6: So Moses accepted the carts and the oxen and gave them to the Levites.
net.NUM_7_7: He gave two carts and four oxen to the Gershonites, as their service required;
net.NUM_7_8: and he gave four carts and eight oxen to the Merarites, as their service required, under the authority of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
net.NUM_7_9: But to the Kohathites he gave none, because the service of the holy things, which they carried on their shoulders, was their responsibility. The Time of Presentation
net.NUM_7_10: The leaders offered gifts for the dedication of the altar when it was anointed. And the leaders presented their offering before the altar.
net.NUM_7_11: For the Lord said to Moses, “They must present their offering, one leader for each day, for the dedication of the altar.” The Tribal Offerings
net.NUM_7_12: The one who presented his offering on the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab, from the tribe of Judah.
net.NUM_7_13: His offering was one silver platter weighing 130 shekels, and one silver sprinkling bowl weighing 70 shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel, each of them full of fine flour mixed with olive oil as a grain offering;
net.NUM_7_14: one gold pan weighing 10 shekels, full of incense;
net.NUM_7_15: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb in its first year, for a burnt offering;
net.NUM_7_16: one male goat for a purification offering;
net.NUM_7_17: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings: two bulls, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs in their first year. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
net.NUM_7_18: On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar, leader of Issachar, presented an offering.
net.NUM_7_19: He offered for his offering one silver platter weighing 130 shekels and one silver sprinkling bowl weighing 70 shekels, both according to the sanctuary shekel, each of them full of fine flour mixed with olive oil as a grain offering;
net.NUM_7_20: one gold pan weighing 10 shekels, full of incense;
net.NUM_7_21: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb in its first year, for a burnt offering;
net.NUM_7_22: one male goat for a purification offering;
net.NUM_7_23: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings: two bulls, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs in their first year. This was the offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.
